description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a visibly weathered texture, suggesting frequent use or age. The axe head is forged from metal, displaying a dark, mottled patina with signs of oxidation and wear, especially along the cutting edge. The blade is broad and wedge-shaped, with a single sharp edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping tasks. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, likely using a traditional wedged fitting, with the top of the handle slightly protruding above the axe head. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding, emphasizing function over form. This type of axe would commonly be found in urban garages, maintenance sheds, or park service areas for chopping wood or clearing brush.
description: A weathered hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, aged metal blade, showing signs of heavy use. The utilitarian design features a single sharp edge and a securely mounted head.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the oval cross-section of the handle and the mounting of the blade onto the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the handle's curvature and the blade's profile, with more emphasis on the blade's edge.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view focusing on the end of the handle, revealing its rounded, unfinished end and the alignment with the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with an oval cross-section, visibly weathered, no added grip features, traditional style.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll and no decorative features; utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle protruding through the axe head.
